output.summary: Six weeks in we're +0.70% alpha vs SPY but -0.85% absolute, with a 33% weekly win rate masking one monster win (MU +59%) and a string of premature OPENs that closed -5% to -14%. Reflection-driven discipline (TLH on MSFT/AVGO/SMCI, trimming UNH on RSI extension, sitting out the WMT/AVGO insider-dump setups) is working; the misses are concentrated in new positions opened into RSI extension or right before adverse macro turns.
what_worked: High-conviction calls outperformed: 6 high-conviction picks averaged +7.2% with 50% win rate vs medium-conviction at -1.8%/21% — sizing up the cleanest cross-signal name (MU 5/25 OPEN → +59.5%) is the single biggest P&L driver.

Insider-dump avoid rule: passing on WMT (Walton $313M/$535M), AVGO post-CTO-sale, AAPL pre-Levinson sell, META cluster sells has been consistently right; every flagged exit-liquidity name we skipped underperformed.

Tax-loss harvesting executed cleanly: MSFT/AVGO/SMCI/AAPL/AMZN/GOOGL/BRK.B/XLE closes captured ~$120 in tax credits and freed capital before further drawdown (AVGO went -14.5%, MSFT -11.8% by close week).
Rotation read on 6/22 (XLI ADD + IWM OPEN, trim UNH) generated +1.38% alpha in a down-SPY week — cyclical breadth call was correct.
what_missed: Cold-start 5/18 deployment: 4 of 5 OPENs (XLE -5.5%, BRK.B -4.1%, GOOGL -4.8%, AMZN -0.5%) closed red within 7-14 days. Opening 6 medium-conviction positions in one week diluted edge — should have staged in.

Chasing breakouts into RSI extension: MSFT OPEN at composite-82 on 6/1 → -11.8% in one week; AVGO OPEN same week → -14.5%. Both had RSI 75+ across the tape and we ignored the 'don't add into extension' rule we later codified.
SMCI 5/25 OPEN at RSI 80 'high conviction' was a chase — closed -12.9% on 6/15. Technical 91 + news 96 was the top of the parabola, not a fresh setup.

Medium-conviction OPENs are net unprofitable (-1.8% avg, 21% win rate). The system is generating too many medium-conviction new positions vs. either passing or sizing up — the middle is where edge dies.
lessons_for_pm: Be ruthless on RSI extension for new OPENs. Last 6 weeks: every OPEN with RSI ≥75 at entry (MSFT, AVGO, SMCI) closed down 11–15% within 14 days. If RSI ≥70 and news ≥90, the setup is exit liquidity not entry — HOLD existing, do NOT initiate.

Concentrate, don't sprinkle. Medium-conviction OPENs are -1.8% avg with 21% win rate; high-conviction are +7.2% with 50% win rate. If the call isn't high-conviction, prefer HOLD/NONE or upsize an existing winner instead of opening a new medium position.
Macro_mismatch remains a hard veto and is paying off — keep avoiding XLU/XLP/XLE/COST/XLY when flagged regardless of news polarity.

Insider exit-liquidity rule is robust: any name with >$30M cluster sales or C-suite dump (WMT, AVGO pre-close, AAPL Levinson, MSFT pre-close, META) — skip or close even if news/technicals look strong.
TLH discipline is adding value — continue harvesting losers ≥$30 tax savings when thesis is broken; don't average down on degraded_signal names.
Don't deploy a full sleeve in one week. Staging in 2–3 positions per week beats 6-name cold-starts; the 5/18 basket lost 4-of-5 because we deployed into a pre-correction tape.
watch_for: MU earnings aftermath: if RSI >70 post-print, do NOT add — book gains on extension per the documented pattern.
Cyclical rotation (XLI/IWM/XLF) follow-through — if breadth fails this week, the 6/22 thesis needs to be re-examined rather than doubled-down.
Mega-cap tech (NVDA/AAPL/MSFT) re-acceleration risk: we're underweight; if cross-signals re-align with RSI <65, that's the high-conviction add window we keep missing.

output.summary: Six weeks in we're +0.70% alpha vs SPY but -0.85% absolute, with a 33% weekly win rate masking one monster win (MU +59%) and a string of premature OPENs that closed -5% to -14%. Reflection-driven discipline (TLH on MSFT/AVGO/SMCI, trimming UNH on RSI extension, sitting out the WMT/AVGO insider-dump setups) is working; the misses are concentrated in new positions opened into RSI extension or right before adverse macro turns.

decisions.thesis: TLH harvests ~$48 tax savings; bear MA stack, $186M director sale, and -9.5% 1M confirm broken thesis — don't average down a falling AI leader.
High-vol semi caught in AI digestion (-3.6% 1W); cut sleeve in half to free capital for regime-favored rotation winners.
Massive earnings beat intact but -7% 1W on lawsuit news and high-vol flag — hold, don't add into mixed signals per watch-for note.
Technicals strong (+10% 1M, bull stack) but news polarity -0.41 — small position, let technicals lead but no add until news divergence resolves.
Top technical score, captures rotation theme; RSI 64 nearing extension so hold rather than add.
Regime-favored financials, bull stack, RSI 63, low vol — upsize the existing winner per 'concentrate don't sprinkle' lesson.
Industrials rotation: bull stack, +5.2% 1M, news polarity 0.91, macro-favored — add to existing winner.
Broadening-rally thesis intact (RSI 60, polarity 0.81); watch breadth follow-through before adding.
Top-ranked candidate with technical breakout + 0.945 news polarity in regime-favored financials; size moderate due to RSI 65 extension risk.
Strong setup but already heavy in XLF/V financials sleeve — avoid sector over-concentration.
Wash-sale block until 7/2 — hard veto on opening per tax discipline.

output.weekly_thesis: Lean into the cyclical rotation: harvest NVDA tax loss, trim AMD, and concentrate fresh capital into financials/industrials (V, XLF, XLI) where cross-signals align.
output.narrative:
Regime is a clear rotation tape — mega-cap tech in correction, financials/industrials/healthcare bid. I'm closing NVDA to capture ~$48 TLH and remove a falling-thesis name with insider selling, and trimming AMD to lower high-vol semi exposure. Freed capital plus existing cash deploys into V (new high-conviction financial breakout) and adds to XLF/XLI winners per the 'concentrate, don't sprinkle' lesson. Holding MU (post-earnings, no chase) and UNH (technicals carrying despite news divergence), avoiding all macro_mismatch and bear-tape mega-caps.</narrative>
<parameter name="target_cash_pct">15
